Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof [ Ccp Sec 2033.280(B) And (C)]
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff PETER J. CROSBY (Tel: 510-701-1843) in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PETER CROSBY's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions [CCP Sec 2033.280(B) And(C)].

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is granted and denied in part. No proof of service of summons was filed. Therefore, the Court cannot determine whether the December 2, 2021 RFAs were served before Defendant was served with the Summons (Defendant filed an answer on February 28, 2022). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Defendant David Wiseblood is ordered to serve verified, objection-free responses to RFAs, Set One, within two weeks of notice of this order. This order shall be served by overnight mail. The Motion to Have the RFAs Deemed Admitted is denied, without prejudice. Lawyers or other self-represented litigants (appearing on their own behalf) cannot recover attorney fees as discovery sanctions. Argaman v. Ratan (1999) 73 CA4th 1173, 1179; Kravitz v. Sup.Ct. (Milner) (2001) 91 CA4th 1015, 1021. Therefore, the request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
